condition: player.docked and player.location == "sorrow_station" and not ("talked_tib_station" in player.flags)

start.text: Didn't expect to see you back at the bar this quick. Something break, or are you just tired of your own cooking?

choices.text: Both. Let me buy you one.
Need a read on the patrol schedules out past Verne.

buy_round.text: I don't turn down free drinks. But fair warning — I drink slow and I talk slower.

choices.text: That's fine. I'm in no rush.

companionable.prompt_hint: You and the captain share a quiet drink at the Sorrow Station bar. You're off duty. Respond as Tib: guarded but not hostile, letting a little warmth through because the silence between you feels earned. The station's ambient noise fills the gaps.

intel.text: Compact's been running more patrols since the dustup at the Veil. Verne approach is hot — they've got a picket line three klicks off the beacon. I'd come in from the nadir, burn quiet, and hope they're watching the wrong screen.

duskway.text: Tove's territory, not mine. She knows those lanes the way I know this ship's torque curve. Ask her — but don't push. She shares what she shares.

mutations.text: The captain asked about the Duskway. I pointed them to Tove. Good to know they're thinking about routes before they need them.

talk.prompt_hint: The captain says they just want to talk. No agenda. You're at the Sorrow Station bar. Respond as Tib: quiet, a little uncomfortable with open-ended conversation, but willing to sit with it. You don't offer much — you let the silence do the work. A crack shows through when you mention the station reminds you of a place you knew before.

mutations.text: Sat with the captain at the Sorrow Station bar. They didn't want anything. Just company. That's rarer than a clean approach vector.

end.text: I'll be at the ship when you're ready to go. Don't leave the approach burn to me — you fly better when you're in the seat.

choices.text: Keep an eye on things here.
